howItWorks.intro: This college savings calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Future Cost = Sum of annual cost × (1+inflation)^years for each year
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: How much does college cost?
faq.answer: In 2026, average annual costs are: public in-state ~$25,000, public out-of-state ~$45,000, private ~$60,000. These include tuition, room, board, and fees.

faq.question: Why does college inflation matter?
faq.answer: College costs have historically risen 5-8% per year — faster than general inflation. A school costing $35K today may cost $57K in 10 years at 5% inflation.

faq.question: What is a 529 plan?
faq.answer: A tax-advantaged savings account for education. Contributions grow tax-free and withdrawals for qualified education expenses are tax-free. Many states offer a state tax deduction too.

faq.question: What if my child gets scholarships?
faq.answer: Great — extra 529 funds can be used for graduate school, transferred to siblings, or rolled into a Roth IRA (up to $35K lifetime, starting 2024).

faq.question: Is the College Savings Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the College Savings Calculator?
sections.body: The College Savings Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your child's current age, annual college cost, years of college, you get instant results including total needed, gap after current savings grow, monthly savings needed. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting total needed right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your child's current age and need to find the right total needed. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Child's Current Age
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your child's current age — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: annual college cost, years of college, college inflation, investment return, already saved. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your total needed, gap after current savings grow, monthly savings needed. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your child's current age before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both total needed and gap after current savings grow — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to manage your finances.
If you're unsure about your already saved, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
